basics.name: David (Jiachen) Weng
basics.label: Community Engagement & Program Coordinator
basics.summary: Community engagement professional with 4+ years designing and delivering co-curricular programs, managing budgets, and building cross-campus partnerships at UBC. Bachelor of Media Studies graduate with firsthand understanding of the BMS program's pedagogical approach and community-engaged learning values. Experienced in event programming for 1,500+ residents, grant-funded budget management ($10K+), cross-cultural facilitation, and educational content development. Bilingual (English & Mandarin) with strong cross-cultural sensitivity.

work.name: BC + AI Ecosystem
work.summary: Lead visual content operations for a growing AI community in British Columbia.
highlights: Designed 50+ branded assets for social media, events, and presentations reaching 3,000+ cumulative viewers
Managed multi-platform content publishing workflow across LinkedIn, Instagram, and WeChat, maintaining brand consistency across 3 channels

work.name: UBC Department of Physical Therapy
work.summary: Designed and developed inclusive e-learning content for the Physical Therapy program, applying Universal Design for Learning principles.
highlights: Designed and developed 5+ inclusive e-learning modules using Articulate 360 (Rise, Storyline), applying Universal Design for Learning (UDL) principles for diverse learner needs
Collaborated with faculty to translate curriculum content into interactive digital formats serving 200+ students, contributing to the program's experiential learning goals
Conducted accessibility audits on educational materials ensuring WCAG 2.1 compliance, and trained faculty on e-learning best practices for independent content management

work.name: UBC Student Housing and Hospitality Services
work.summary: Planned and delivered community-engaged learning programs for a residence community of 1,500+ students, combining program development, peer education, budget management, and safety administration.
highlights: Designed and executed 12+ residential programs per academic year — including cross-cultural celebrations, educational workshops, and peer mentoring activities — engaging 25–60+ attendees per event

Built and stewarded relationships with 1,500+ residents through structured engagement protocols (unit visits, wellness check-ins, peer-to-peer support), fostering a supportive and inclusive living environment
Managed program budgets ($10–15/resident allocation), authorizing expenditures and submitting financial reports through Workday in compliance with university regulations
Facilitated cross-cultural co-curricular programming (Mahjong Night, Around the World in Games, Wear Your Pride) promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion among diverse resident populations
Designed and distributed monthly community newsletters, improving resident awareness of available resources and upcoming events
Coordinated with Resident Life team, campus services, and external partners to deliver educational resource events connecting students with financial literacy and support services
Administered weekly duty shifts including late-night safety rounds, enforcing campus policy and emergency response protocols for a community of 1,500+ residents

work.name: Citang (词堂)
work.summary: Create, contextualize, and curate internet culture content with cultural and sociological commentary for a bilingual audience.
highlights: Created and contextualized internet culture content, driving 150,090 views on a single post and contributing to 5,000+ follower growth over 6 months
Curated and annotated meme content with cross-cultural commentary, serving bilingual (CN/EN) audience needs
Monitored trending topics and cultural phenomena to produce timely, relevant content in a fast-moving digital landscape

volunteer.summary: Managed all financial activities for the Media Studies Student Association, including budget administration, grant applications, and financial reporting.
highlights: Managed association budget and financial operations over 2-year term, including authorizing expenditures, reconciling statements, and ensuring compliance with funding body regulations
Collaborated with VP Events to plan and allocate budgets across co-curricular activities, balancing financial constraints with program quality and student engagement goals
Presented quarterly financial reports to executive team and membership, maintaining transparency and accountability in fund management

volunteer.summary: Developed and executed communication strategies for student association events and programming.
highlights: Developed and executed communication and marketing strategies for 8+ annual events, including web-based newsletters and social media content reaching 200+ members
Co-authored monthly newsletters and managed distribution lists, driving event awareness and student participation in co-curricular programming

volunteer.organization: UBC Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA)
volunteer.summary: Directed all media production for UBC's Chinese Students and Scholars Association, serving 1,000+ community members.
highlights: Cultivated cross-campus relationships with 5+ UBC units to facilitate community engagement collaborations, serving 1,000+ international students through 10+ annual cultural events
Led media team of 8+ volunteers, coordinating event photography, videography, and content production to support program outreach and engagement

volunteer.summary: Managed social media strategy for a Vancouver-based arts nonprofit.
highlights: Managed social media strategy and execution across 2+ platforms, increasing follower engagement by 25%+
Developed and maintained content calendar, producing 20+ branded posts per month aligned with organizational campaigns
Crafted engaging articles for monthly newsletter, driving subscriber retention and event attendance

awards.title: Faculty of Arts International Student Scholarship

publications.name: Transcending Barriers: Viewing Practices of 'Games of Thrones' in China, India, and Russia
publications.publisher: Beacon: UBC Journal of Media Studies
publications.summary: Cross-cultural media consumption research examining unauthorized viewing practices across three countries.

projects.name: OpenKara
projects.description: Open-source desktop karaoke application using AI vocal separation and online synced lyrics.
highlights: Built with Tauri 2 + Rust, demonstrating technical project management and product development skills
